title: Reasoning to Attend: Try to Understand How Token Works
abstract: This paper explores reasoning segmentation, an extension of Referring Expression Segmentation (RES) that outputs nuanced masks for implicitly referred objects given descriptive language expressions. The study focuses on understanding how the <SEG> token works when mapping language vocabulary embedding into visual space.
key_findings: <SEG> token, despite lacking inherent semantics, can ground objects within images when inserted into conversation templates and jointly trained with LMMs
Consistency in activation responses across similarity maps suggests <SEG> token bridges semantic connections between textual prompts and visual correspondences
contributions: Analysis of how <SEG> token works in mapping language to visual space
Visualization of similarity maps showing semantic connections
Understanding the role of <SEG> token in bridging textual and visual domains
